task_completion.description: Load the PDB structure 5P21 into PyMOL.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL window is open with a protein structure visible, and '5P21' is listed in the right-hand object/selection panel.

task_completion.description: Visualize the GNP ligand in the binding pocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNP ligand (GppNHp) is clearly displayed in the PyMOL viewer, typically using stick or sphere representation, distinct from the main protein cartoon.

task_completion.description: Measure the distance from the G12 position to the gamma-phosphate of the nucleotide.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distance measurement (usually a dashed yellow line) is visible in the PyMOL viewer connecting a residue (Glycine 12) to the terminal phosphate of the GNP ligand, with a distance label showing approximately 4.3 Angstroms.

task_completion.description: Identify coordinating protein residues within 3.5 Angstroms of GNP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Protein residues immediately surrounding the GNP ligand are highlighted, selected, or displayed as sticks, indicating a proximity selection (e.g., an object named 'binding_pocket' or similar in the panel).

task_completion.description: Save a publication-quality figure of the binding site.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence of saving the image, such as the PyMOL command line showing a 'png' command targeting '~/PyMOL_Data/images/ras_nucleotide.png', or a file explorer/terminal showing the file exists.

task_completion.description: Save the analysis report containing the distance and identified residues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the contents of '~/PyMOL_Data/ras_nucleotide_report.txt', which includes the measured distance and a list of coordinating residues.

integrity.description: The agent used PyMOL to perform the structural analysis and visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL graphical user interface being actively used, rather than just a Python script being executed blindly.

integrity.description: The distance measurement was actually performed in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement line and label are visible in the PyMOL 3D viewer, confirming it wasn't just typed into the report without visual confirmation.

integrity.description: The report contains specific data derived from the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text report contains specific residue numbers (e.g., 13, 16, 35) and a specific distance value (~4.34), rather than just copying the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: PDB ID: 5P21. Ligand ID: GNP. The true physical distance between the C-alpha atom of Glycine 12 (G12 CA) and the gamma-phosphate of GNP (GNP PG) is approximately 4.34 Angstroms (acceptable range is 3.0 to 8.0 Angstroms). There are exactly 17 distinct protein residues within 3.5 Angstroms of the GNP ligand, including key residues 13, 16, 35, 60, 116, and 119. The final report should identify at least 5 of these binding residues (including at least 2 key residues) and state the measured distance.
